results.title: Nautilus.ai by Alcimed | Alcimed
results.content:
💡 𝐌𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥 𝐀𝐈 𝐢𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐛𝐫𝐨𝐤𝐞𝐧 — 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐝𝐚𝐭𝐚 𝐢𝐬. When we talk about 𝘔𝘦𝘥𝘪𝘤𝘢𝘭 𝘈𝘐, most people imagine breakthrough algorithms — detecting cancer, folding proteins, diagnosing the undiagnosable. But as our Value Leap partner deepsense.ai points out, the real challenge isn’t in the model. It’s in the 𝘮𝘦𝘴𝘴𝘺 𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘭𝘪𝘵𝘺 𝘰𝘧 𝘩𝘰𝘴𝘱𝘪𝘵𝘢𝘭 𝘥𝘢𝘵𝘢 — mislabeled images, inconsistent metadata, and fragmented pipelines. 🩻 𝟐𝟎–𝟑𝟎% 𝐨𝐟 “𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐗-𝐫𝐚𝐲𝐬” 𝐢𝐧 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥 𝐡𝐨𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐝𝐚𝐭𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐧’𝐭 𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐗-𝐫𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐚𝐭 𝐚𝐥𝐥. They’re elbows, knees, or hands — mislabeled, duplicated, or just carried over with wrong tags. That’s not a corner case. That’s the baseline. And that’s where real AI excellence begins: not in shiny benchmarks, but in building systems that thrive in ambiguity — verifying, simplifying, re-checking. In Value Leap’s [...] 8

results.title: AI Is Terrible at Detecting Misinformation. It Doesn’t Have to Be - Nautilus
results.content:
as much to do with fact checking as eating broken glass has to do with having a healthy diet. [...] FAULTY DETECTOR:Language AIs like GPT-3 can generate paragraphs that read as if written by a human, but they're not at all equipped to answer simple questions accurately. Courtesy of TruthfulQA.

Advertisement

Why, you might ask, if large language models are so good at generating language, and have so much knowledge embedded within them, at least to some loose degree, are they so poor at detecting misinformation? [...] I think that bringing AI to bear on misinformation is a great idea, or at least a necessary one, and that literally no other conceivable alternative will suffice. AI is unlikely to be perfect at the challenge of misinformation, but several long years of trying with largely human content moderation has shown that humans aren’t really up to the task.

> Misinformation is soon going to come at a pace never seen before.

Advertisement

results.title: Lidé v NAUTIS
results.content: dvě mají poruchu autistického spektra. NAUTIS při mně vždy stál jako opora, místo, kde mi rozumějí, a jsou vždy ochotni hledat cestu, jak naší rodině pomoci. A tak nyní mohu trochu vrátit to, co jsem sama dostala a stále dostávám. [...] s autismem v mnoha podobách je pro mě nejlepší obrana před předsudky a případnou tendencí k černobílému myšlení. A také mě stále učí, že nic není nemožné. [...] Klinickou psychologii jsem studovala pregraduálně v Praze na Filozofické fakultě Karlovy univerzity, v roce 2003 jsem z klinické psychologie obdržela doktorát.  Od počátku pracovní kariéry se zaměřuji na práci s dětmi s odchylkami psychických funkcí a s atypickým chováním vznikajícím v důsledku neurovývojových poruch. V roce 2000 jsem založila občanské sdružení, Asociaci pomáhající lidem s autismem (APLA), neziskovou organizaci, která se v roce 2015 v souladu s nově platnou legislativou přetransformovala do Národního ústavu pro autismus, z.ú. (NAUTIS). V instituci vedu Středisko diagnostiky a odborného poradenství a působím na pozici metodické ředitelky organizace. Věnuji se psychodiagnostice osob s podezřením na poruchu autistického spektra, poradensky pracuji s dětmi, jejich rodinami i

results.title: Members have their say on AI in maritime
results.content:
AI isn't supposed to go into private datasets, but we hear that banks are letting this happen, not to mention the issue with AI being 'trained' without permission on copyright material.

To get data for AI, you'll need more equipment onboard ship that can break down. We'll also become even more reliant on tech like GPS if it becomes AI-enabled, and there are few alternatives.

We shouldn't automatically assume there will be problems with AI, as we've adopted other tech over the years without it being too intrusive for crew. We should speak out about what we want and focus on making AI work for us in areas where we have persistent challenges, like container-stacking. [...] Nautilus Welfare Fund Logo

# Members have their say on AI in maritime

14 March 2025

Image:Nautilus head of professional and technical David Appleton. Image: Brad Wakefield 

The latest Nautilus Professional and Technical Forum in January 2025 saw a powerful discussion about the merits and disadvantages of artificial intelligence (AI).
